problem: Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease?
mechanism: mRNA therapy to upregulate/downregulate gene expression
hypothesis: The key to Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease? is mRNA therapy to upregulate/downregulate gene expression — specifically mRNA therapy to — which would help 50,000,000 people affected.

experiment.protocol: In vitro cell culture assay with 34 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: The art of science is asking the right question, not answering. — Max Planck paraphrase
